title: Sharded full-text search index
description: Defines the shape of dist/assets/search-vocab.json and dist/assets/search-shard-NN.json, the lazy-loaded inverted index that powers free-text search in the SPA. The index is built from every Use Case in the catalogue at build time and ships as a static asset; the client downloads the vocabulary on first keystroke and pulls only the shards relevant to the typed query. Renaming or removing fields requires bumping the major component of `version` and a full deprecation cycle (see docs/api-versioning.md).
x-stability-note: v7.0 ships this surface as preview while the search shard pipeline beds in. Promotion to `stable` happens at v7.1.

version.description: Format version. Bumping this forces the client to invalidate every cached shard.

shardCount.description: Number of shard files. Currently locked at 16; raising this is a breaking change because shard filenames listed in shardFiles become orphaned in browser caches.

hash.description: Algorithm used to route a token to a shard: shard_id = fnv1a32(utf8(token)) % shardCount. FNV-1a chosen over BLAKE2b because the JS implementation is ~7 lines vs ~150 with no dependence on Web Crypto. Distribution is uniform to within 5% across our 12k-token vocabulary. Changing this also invalidates every cached shard.

ucIds.description: Compact docid → UC id mapping. Posting lists in shards reference UCs by their index into this array, not by their string id, to keep posting JSON small.

tokens.description: Sorted full vocabulary. Used by the client to expand prefix queries (e.g. 'auth' → 'auth, authorize, authenticate, authentication') and to decide which shards to fetch for a given query.

shardFiles.description: Per-shard fingerprinted filename, indexed by shard id. The vocab keeps a stable filename so the SPA can fetch it cold; shards carry a content hash so the client can cache them forever and still get coherent updates when the catalogue is rebuilt.

shard.description: Shard id; matches the NN in the filename.

postings.description: Token → posting list. Posting lists are sorted compact docids (indices into vocab.ucIds), comma-joined as a string. Storing as a string saves ~30% over JSON arrays once gzipped because there are no [] bracket overheads per row.
